controls.control_objective: To ensure be necessary, reserve bank of india also issues directions to authorised persons under section 11 of the foreign exchange management act (fema), 199
controls.control_description:
To operationalize the Regulations as may be necessary, Reserve Bank of India also issues 
directions to Authorised Persons under Section 11 of the Foreign Exchange Management Act 
(FEMA), 1999.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be necessary, reserve bank of india also issues directions to authorised persons under section 11 of the foreign exchange management act (fema), 199

controls.control_objective: To ensure issue directions to authorised persons through a
controls.control_description:
It may be noted that, whenever necessary, Reserve Bank shall issue directions to Authorised 
Persons through A.P.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to issue directions to authorised persons through a

controls.control_objective: To ensure be amended suitably simultaneously
controls.control_description:
The Master Direction issued herewith shall be amended suitably 
simultaneously.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be amended suitably simultaneously

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain a foreign currency account outside india if he had maintained it when he was resident outside india or inherited it from a person resident o
controls.control_description:
1.3 A person resident in India may maintain a foreign currency account outside India if he had 
maintained it when he was resident outside India or inherited it from a person resident outside 
India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain a foreign currency account outside india if he had maintained it when he was resident outside india or inherited it from a person resident o

controls.control_objective: To ensure a person authorised as an authorised dealer under sub- section (1) of section 10 of fema
controls.control_description:
Definitions 
 
Some key terms used in this part are given below: 
 
2.1 ‘Authorised Dealer’ (AD) means a person authorised as an authorised dealer under sub-
section (1) of section 10 of FEMA.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to a person authorised as an authorised dealer under sub- section (1) of section 10 of fema

controls.control_objective: To ensure an account held or maintained in currency other than the currency of india or nepal or bhutan
controls.control_description:
2.2 ‘Foreign Currency Account’ means an account held or maintained in currency other than the 
currency of India or Nepal or Bhutan.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to an account held or maintained in currency other than the currency of india or nepal or bhutan

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used for the following purposes: a
controls.control_description:
The loan cannot be repatriated outside India and shall be used for the 
following purposes:
a.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used for the following purposes: a

controls.control_objective: To ensure be no direct or indirect foreign exchange consideration for the non-resident depositor agreeing to pledge his deposits to enable the resident indivi
controls.control_description:
In case of loans sanctioned to a third party, there should be no direct or indirect foreign 
exchange consideration for the non-resident depositor agreeing to pledge his deposits to enable 
the resident individual/ firm/ company to obtain such facilities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be no direct or indirect foreign exchange consideration for the non-resident depositor agreeing to pledge his deposits to enable the resident indivi

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow their branches/ correspondents outside india to grant loans to or in favour of non-resident depositor or to third parties at the request of depositor for bona fide purpose against the security of funds held in the nre accounts in india and also agree for remittance of the funds from india
controls.control_description:
4.8.2 Authorised Dealers may allow their branches/ correspondents outside India to grant loans 
to or in favour of non-resident depositor or to third parties at the request of depositor for bona 
fide purpose against the security of funds held in the NRE accounts in India and also agree for 
remittance of the funds from India, if necessary, for liquidation of the outstanding.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to allow their branches/ correspondents outside india to grant loans to or in favour of non-resident depositor or to third parties at the request of depositor for bona fide purpose against the security of funds held in the nre accounts in india and also agree for remittance of the funds from india

controls.control_objective: To ensure include all types of fund based/ non-fund based facilities
controls.control_description: 4.8.4 The term “loan” shall include all types of fund based/ non-fund based facilities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include all types of fund based/ non-fund based facilities

controls.control_objective: To ensure be transferred to the rfc accounts, at the option of the account holder, immediately upon the return of the account holder to india for taking up emp
controls.control_description:
4.9 NRE accounts should be designated as resident accounts or the funds held in these 
accounts may be transferred to the RFC accounts, at the option of the account holder, 
immediately upon the return of the account holder to India for taking up employment or on 
change in the residential status.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be transferred to the rfc accounts, at the option of the account holder, immediately upon the return of the account holder to india for taking up emp

controls.control_objective: To ensure 5, 2016
controls.control_description: 27 Inserted by AP (DIR Series) circular No 67/2015-16/ [(1)/5(R)] dated May 5, 2016.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to 5, 2016

controls.control_objective: To ensure be available where loans against such deposits are availed of
controls.control_description:
The facility for premature withdrawal of the 
deposits shall not be available where loans against such deposits are availed of.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be available where loans against such deposits are availed of

controls.control_objective: To ensure include all types of fund based/ non-fund based facilities
controls.control_description:
The term “loan” shall include all types of fund 
based/ non-fund based facilities.”
15
account holder or for similar other purposes, should be forwarded to the Reserve Bank for 
consideration.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include all types of fund based/ non-fund based facilities

controls.control_objective: To ensure be allowed in terms of power of attorney or other authority granted in favour of a resident by the non-resident account holder
controls.control_description:
4.11 Operations on an NRE account may be allowed in terms of Power of Attorney or other 
authority granted in favour of a resident by the non-resident account holder, provided such 
operations are restricted to withdrawals for local payments or remittance to the account holder 
himself through banking channels.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be allowed in terms of power of attorney or other authority granted in favour of a resident by the non-resident account holder

controls.control_objective: To ensure be permitted to operate the account to facilitate such investment
controls.control_description:
In cases where the account holder or a bank designated by 
him is eligible to make investments in India, the Power of Attorney holder may be permitted to 
operate the account to facilitate such investment.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be permitted to operate the account to facilitate such investment

controls.control_objective: To ensure be accepted in any permissible currency
controls.control_description: Deposits may be accepted in any permissible currency.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be accepted in any permissible currency

controls.control_objective: To ensure open and maintain nro account with an authorised dealer or an authorised bank for the purpose of putting through bona fide transactions denominated
controls.control_description:
The salient features of the scheme are given below: 
 
6.1 Any person resident outside India (as per Section 2 (w) of FEMA), may open and maintain 
NRO account with an Authorised Dealer or an Authorised Bank for the purpose of putting 
through bona fide transactions denominated in Indian Rupees.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to open and maintain nro account with an authorised dealer or an authorised bank for the purpose of putting through bona fide transactions denominated

controls.control_objective: To ensure to: a) comply with the directions issued by the reserve bank from time to time
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to: a) comply with the directions issued by the reserve bank from time to time

controls.control_objective: To ensure be stipulated by the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
and 
 
b) submit periodic return or statement, if any, as may be stipulated by the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be stipulated by the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure open/close rupee accounts (non-interest bearing) in the name of their overseas branches or correspondents without prior reference to the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Rupee Vostro Accounts of Banks 
 
AD Category-I banks may open/close Rupee accounts (non-interest bearing) in the name of 
their overseas branches or correspondents without prior reference to the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to open/close rupee accounts (non-interest bearing) in the name of their overseas branches or correspondents without prior reference to the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure freely purchase foreign currency from their overseas correspondents/ branches at on-going market rates to lay down funds in their accounts for meeti
controls.control_description:
1.1 Funding of Accounts of Non-resident Banks 
 
(i) AD Category I banks may freely purchase foreign currency from their overseas 
correspondents/ branches at on-going market rates to lay down funds in their accounts for 
meeting their bonafide needs in India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to freely purchase foreign currency from their overseas correspondents/ branches at on-going market rates to lay down funds in their accounts for meeti

controls.control_objective: To ensure be closely monitored to ensure that overseas banks do not take a speculative view on the indian rupee (inr)
controls.control_description:
(ii)  Transactions in the accounts should be closely monitored to ensure that overseas banks do 
not take a speculative view on the Indian Rupee (INR).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be closely monitored to ensure that overseas banks do not take a speculative view on the indian rupee (inr)

controls.control_objective: To ensure be notified to the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Any such instances should be notified to 
the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be notified to the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be freely converted into foreign currency
controls.control_description:
1.3 Conversion of Rupees into Foreign Currencies 
 
Balances held in Rupee accounts of non-resident banks may be freely converted into foreign 
currency.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be freely converted into foreign currency

controls.control_objective: To ensure be recorded in form a2 and the corresponding debit to the account should be in form a3 under the relevant returns
controls.control_description:
All such transactions should be recorded in Form A2 and the corresponding debit to 
the account should be in form A3 under the relevant Returns.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be recorded in form a2 and the corresponding debit to the account should be in form a3 under the relevant returns

controls.control_objective: To ensure be
controls.control_description:
1.4 Responsibilities of Paying and Receiving Banks 
 
In the case of credit to accounts the paying banker should ensure that all regulatory 
requirements are met and are correctly furnished in form A1/A2 as the case may be.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be

controls.control_objective: To ensure be complied with without reference to reserve bank after satisfying themselves that the refunds are not being made in cover of transactions of compe
controls.control_description:
1.5 Refund of Rupee Remittances 
 
Requests for cancellation or refund of inward remittances may be complied with without 
reference to Reserve Bank after satisfying themselves that the refunds are not being made in 
cover of transactions of compensatory nature.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be complied with without reference to reserve bank after satisfying themselves that the refunds are not being made in cover of transactions of compe

controls.control_objective: To ensure permit their overseas branches/ correspondents temporary overdrawals not exceeding rs
controls.control_description:
1.6 Overdrafts/Loans to overseas Branches/Correspondents 
 
(i) AD Category-I banks may permit their overseas branches/ correspondents temporary 
overdrawals not exceeding Rs.500 lakhs in aggregate, for meeting normal business 
requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to permit their overseas branches/ correspondents temporary overdrawals not exceeding rs

controls.control_objective: To ensure be used to postpone funding of accounts
controls.control_description: This facility should not be used to postpone funding of accounts.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be used to postpone funding of accounts

controls.control_objective: To ensure be submitted to the chief general manager-in-charge, foreign exchange department, reserve bank of india, central office,11th floor, central office b
controls.control_description:
If overdrafts in excess of the 
above limit are not adjusted within five days a report should be submitted to the Chief General 
Manager-in-Charge, Foreign Exchange Department, Reserve Bank of India, Central Office,11th 
Floor, Central Office Building, Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, Fort, Mumbai – 400 001 within 15 
days from the close of the month, stating the reasons thereof.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be submitted to the chief general manager-in-charge, foreign exchange department, reserve bank of india, central office,11th floor, central office b

controls.control_objective: To ensure seek prior approval from the chief general manager-in- charge, foreign exchange department, reserve bank of india, central office
controls.control_description:
(ii) AD Category-I bank wishing to extend any other credit facility in excess of that mentioned at 
(i) above to overseas banks should seek prior approval from the Chief General Manager-in-
Charge, Foreign Exchange Department, Reserve Bank of India, Central Office.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to seek prior approval from the chief general manager-in- charge, foreign exchange department, reserve bank of india, central office

controls.control_objective: To ensure furnish an up-to-date list of all its offices/branches, which are maintaining rupee accounts of non-resident banks as at the end of december every y
controls.control_description:
1.7 Reports to the Reserve Bank 
 
The Head/Principal Office of each AD Category-I banks should furnish an up-to-date list of all its 
offices/branches, which are maintaining Rupee accounts of non-resident banks as at the end of 
December every year giving their code numbers allotted by Reserve Bank through CIMS by 
15th January of the following year.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to furnish an up-to-date list of all its offices/branches, which are maintaining rupee accounts of non-resident banks as at the end of december every y

controls.control_objective: To ensure be classified according to area of jurisdiction of reserve bank offices within which they are situated
controls.control_description:
The offices/branches should be classified according to area 
of jurisdiction of Reserve Bank Offices within which they are situated.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be classified according to area of jurisdiction of reserve bank offices within which they are situated

controls.control_objective: To ensure also open special rupee vostro accounts of correspondent bank/s for settlement of trade transactions of the partner trading country
controls.control_description:
Special Rupee Vostro Account for Banks 
 
AD Category-I banks may also open Special Rupee Vostro Accounts of correspondent bank/s 
for settlement of trade transactions of the partner trading country.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also open special rupee vostro accounts of correspondent bank/s for settlement of trade transactions of the partner trading country
